pages.text:
AUTHOR SEAN RITCHIE
VERSION 4.0
PURPOSE/CHANGES CAS# ADDITION
DATE PUBLISHED JAN 2024
CHARCOAT IC
SAFETY DATA SHEET 2024
Published: Jan 2024
SECTION 1: IDENTIFICATION OF THE SUBSTANCE/MIXTURE AND OF THE COMPANY/UNDERTAKING
1.1 Product Identifier: CharCoat IC
1.2 Relevant identified uses of the substance
or mixture and uses advised against: None
1.2.1 Relevant Uses: Silicon Rubber Coating for Insulator Protection
1.2.2 Uses Advise Against: None Known
1.3 Details of the Supplier of the Safety Data Sheet
Company Address: CharCoat Passive Fire Protection
Suite 301 – 220 Brew Street
Port Moody BC V3H 0H6 Canada
Address Enquiries: Email: mail@CharCoat.com
Technical Information Internet: www.charcoat.com
1.4 Emergency Contact Number: Telephone: +1 604 941 1001 (24 Hours)
SECTION 2: HAZARDS IDENTIFICATION
2.1 Classification of the Substance or Mixture: No classification.
2.2 Label Elements
Hazard Statements: Flammable with toxic fumes.
Skin: May cause irritation.
Eye: Highly irritation with risk of irreversible damage.
Ingestion: Harmful. May cause lung damage if swallowed.
No carcinogenetic, mutagenic, or genetic effects established.
Substances representing health hazard.
2.3 Other Hazards
Environmental Hazards: Flammable with toxic fumes.
Other Hazards: See hazard statement.
SECTION 3: COMPOSITION/INFORMATION ON INGREDIENTS
Product Type: The product is a mixture.
Ingredients (Specific) % by Weight CAS NUMBER
Acetone 28 67-64-1
Toluene 18 108-88-3
Xylene 6 1330-20-7
Silicone Rubber mixture 48 63394-02-5
Comment on Component Parts

pages.text:
SECTION 4: FIRST AID MEASURES
4.1 Description of First Aid Measure
General Information: Remove from fume source, seek medical attention.
Inhalation: Ensure supply of fresh air.
In the event of symptoms seek medical treatment.
Skin Contact: When in contact with the skin, clean with soap and water. Remove
contaminated clothing.
Consult a doctor if skin irritation persists.
Eye Contact: Rinse cautiously (irrigate) with water for several minutes. Remove contact
lenses, if present and easy to do. Continue rinsing. Get medical
advice/attention.
Ingestion: Get medical advice.
Do not induce vomiting.
SECTION 5: FIRE FIGHTING MEASURES
5.1 Extinguishing Media
Suitable Extinguishing Media:
Flammable with toxic fumes. Suitable extinguishing media are: Dry powder, Foam and Carbon Dioxide.
Extinguishing Media that must not be used:
Full water jet.
5.2 Special Hazards arising from the Substance or Mixture:
Can form explosive vapor / air mixture.
5.3 Advice for Fire Fighters:
Use self-contained breathing apparatus. Wear gloves, goggles, and protective overalls. Fire residues and
contaminated firefighting water must be disposed of in accordance with the local regulations.
SECTION 6: ACCIDENTAL RELEASE MEASURES
6.1 Personal Precautions, Protective Equipment, and Emergency Procedures:
Wear gloves, goggles, and protective overalls.
6.2 Environmental Precautions:
Do not discharge into the drains/surface waters/groundwater.
6.3 Methods and Material for Containment and Clean-up:
Use an inert material to contain and collect. Do not take up residues with sawdust.
Dispose of absorbed material in accordance with the regulations.
SECTION 7: HANDLING AND STORAGE
7.1 Precautions for Safe Handling:
Use only in well-ventilated areas.
7.2 Conditions for Safe Storage, including any Incompatibilities:
Keep only in original container in a well-ventilated area. Do not store together with food and animal food/diet.
Keep container tightly closed. Spilled substance increases risk of slipping.
Store away from naked flames.
7.3 Specific End Use(s):
See product use, Section 1.2.
SDS CHARCOAT IC

pages.text:
SECTION 8: EXPOSURE CONTROLS / PERSONAL PROTECTION
8.1 Control Parameters
Ingredients with occupational exposure limits to be monitored (GB): Not applicable.
8.2 Exposure Controls
Additional advice on system design: Ensure adequate ventilation on workstation. Do not smoke when
handling this material. Observe standard industrial hygiene practices with
this material.
Eye Protection: Safety glasses. (EN 166:2001).
Hand Protection: 0,7mm Butyl rubber, >480 min (EN 374).
The details concerned are recommendations. Please contact the glove
supplier for further information.
Skin /Eye Protection: Avoid direct contact with skin and eyes.
Other: Avoid contact with eyes and skin.
Do not breathe vapor/spray.
Respiratory Protection: Breathing apparatus in the event of aerosol or mist formation.
Short term: filter apparatus, combination filter A-P2. (DIN EN 14387)
Thermal Hazards: Not applicable.
Delimitation and monitoring of the
environmental exposition: Comply with applicable environmental regulations limiting discharge to air,
water, and soil.
SECTION 9: PHYSICAL AND CHEMICAL PROPERTIES
9.1 Information on basic physical and chemical properties:
Form Viscous Liquid
Color Light Blue / Gray
Flash Point [°C] ˃23°C
Flammability (solid, gas) [°C] Flammable
9.2 Other Information: None
SECTION 10: STABILITY AND REACTIVITY
10.1 Reactivity: No dangerous reactions known if used as directed.
10.2 Chemical Stability: The product is stable under ambient conditions.
10.3 Conditions to Avoid: See Section 7.
10.4 Hazardous Decomposition Products: Toxic fumes if product is ignited.
SECTION 11: TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
11.1 Information on Toxicological Effects
General Remarks: No carcinogenic, mutagenic, or genetic effects established.
SDS CHARCOAT IC

pages.text:
SECTION 12: ECOLOGICAL INFORMATION
12.1 Toxicity: May have short term environmental effects. Contain, monitor, and remove.
SECTION 13: DISPOSAL CONSIDERATIONS
13.1 Waste Treatment Methods Waste material must be disposed of in accordance with national and local
regulations.
Product: After solidification material can be stored together with domestic waste.
Contaminated Packaging: Packaging that cannot be cleaned should be disposed of as for product.
Uncontaminated packaging may be taken for recycling.
SECTION 14: TRANSPORT INFORMATION
14.1 UN Number U.N. No. 1866 Class 3 Hazard Warning: Flammable / Harmful.
SECTION 15: REGULATORY INFORMATION
15.1 Safety, health, and environmental regulations/legislation specific for the substance or mixture
EEC Regulations: 1991/689 (2001/118); 1999/13; 2004/42; 648/2004; 1907/2006 (REACH);1272/2008;
75/324/EEC (2008/47/EC); 453/2010/EC; (EU) 2015/830
R10 Flammable
R65 Harmful if ingested
S2 Keep out of reach of children
S13 Store remote from foodstuffs and livestock feeds
S43a In case of fire use CO2 or dry foam, do not use water jet
S62 If ingested do not induce vomiting, seek medical attention
SECTION 16: OTHER INFORMATION
16.1 Other Information: Take precautionary measures. Do not use near naked flame. Avoid static discharge. If in doubt
seek medical attention.
SDS CHARCOAT IC
